universalEdges.evidence: Databricks Q1: safety observations co-occur with 30+ trades at 10+ companies each (2yr lookback)
Databricks Q1: any trade can be cited — Electrician, Laborer, Ironworker, Plumber, Carpenter all in top 10
Databricks Q1: incidents span all trade categories present on site
Any trade worker can be the subject of an incident record
Any trade worker on site can witness an incident
Databricks Q2: punch items span all 16 MasterFormat divisions at 5+ companies each
Databricks Q2: every trade division produces punch items — 09 Finishes highest, then 26-28 Electrical
Any trade that receives a punch item must close it out
Inspection items can flag deficiencies in any trade's installed work
NCRs can be issued against any trade whose work deviates from spec
Deficiency notices can be served to any trade responsible for non-compliant work
Quality observations can flag deficient work by any trade
General observations can be recorded for any trade's work area
Corrective actions can be assigned to any trade that performed non-conforming work
Action plans can target remediation by any trade
Action plan tasks can be executed by any trade
Scheduled inspections verify work completed by any trade at milestones
Coordination issues can involve any trade's installed or planned work
Spatial or specification conflicts can occur with any trade's scope
Observation density is a metric calculated per trade
Deficiency rate is computed across all inspections for each trade
Punch item density is compared across all trades
TRIR is tracked for every trade's workforce on site
DART rate applies to every trade with workers on site
Hazard recurrence can occur in any trade's work zone
Rework cost is attributable to corrections for any trade
Resolution time is measured per trade
Pass rate is computed per trade's work scope
Near-miss ratio applies to all trades
Safety observations recorded near any equipment on the jobsite
Incidents can involve any type of construction equipment
Databricks Q2+Q3: punch items span all MasterFormat divisions and material categories
Punch item issues can involve any installed material
NCRs can reject any installed or delivered material that fails spec
Quality observations can flag defects on any installed material
Deficiency notices can identify non-conformance in any material
Inspection items evaluate installed condition of any material
Observations can be recorded for conditions on any material
Rework cost can be caused by failures in any material's installation

verbGlossary.OBSERVED FOR: {source} is recorded involving or near {target}'s work area or crew
verbGlossary.CITED AGAINST: {source} is issued naming {target} or worker as the responsible party
verbGlossary.FILED FOR: {source} is formally logged concerning {target} worker who was affected
verbGlossary.TESTIFIED BY: {source} is provided by {target} worker who observed the event
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED TO: {source} is formally assigned to {target} for correction during closeout
verbGlossary.ATTRIBUTED TO: {source} is traced back to {target} whose work scope caused the deficiency
verbGlossary.CLOSED OUT BY: {source} is completed and signed off by {target} after rework
verbGlossary.FAILED BY: {source} is marked deficient due to work performed by {target}
verbGlossary.ISSUED AGAINST: {source} is formally issued against {target} whose work deviated from spec
verbGlossary.SERVED TO: {source} is formally delivered to {target} responsible for non-compliant work
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED AGAINST: {source} is written up identifying deficient work by {target}
verbGlossary.NOTED FOR: {source} is recorded noting a condition related to {target}'s or material's work area
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED BACK TO: {source} is returned to {target} who performed the non-conforming work
verbGlossary.DIRECTED AT: {source} targets the work area or scope of {target} for remediation
verbGlossary.REMEDIATED BY: {source} is executed by {target} to correct a previously identified deficiency
verbGlossary.SCHEDULED FOR: {source} is set up to verify work completed by {target} at a milestone
verbGlossary.FLAGGED AGAINST: {source} is raised involving {target}'s installed or planned work
verbGlossary.CLASHED WITH: {source} identifies a spatial or specification conflict with {target}'s scope
verbGlossary.MEASURED FOR: {source} is calculated for {target}'s scope across inspections
verbGlossary.HIGHEST AMONG: {source} indicates {target} has the greatest volume of open items
verbGlossary.TRACKED FOR: {source} is monitored over time for {target}'s workforce
verbGlossary.CALCULATED FOR: {source} is computed from lost-time and restricted-duty incidents for {target}
verbGlossary.PERSISTED WITH: {source} indicates the same hazard keeps appearing in {target}'s zone
verbGlossary.INCURRED BY: {source} is attributable to corrections required on work by {target}
verbGlossary.RESOLVED BY: {target} measures how quickly {source} addresses a finding
verbGlossary.SCORED FOR: {source} is computed for {target}'s work scope across inspections
verbGlossary.ELEVATED FOR: {source} is notably higher for {target}, indicating increased risk exposure
verbGlossary.OBSERVED NEAR: {source} is recorded in the vicinity of {target} during operations
verbGlossary.OCCURRED ON: {source} took place on or involving {target} during construction operations
verbGlossary.CATEGORIZED FOR: {source} is classified under a category related to {target}'s installation
verbGlossary.REJECTED FOR: {source} rejects installed or delivered {target} that fails spec
verbGlossary.IDENTIFIED ON: {source} flags a visible defect or concern on {target}
verbGlossary.DEFICIENT IN: {source} identifies {target} as installed does not meet standards
verbGlossary.INSPECTED ON: {target} evaluates the installed condition of {source} for compliance
verbGlossary.DRIVEN BY: {source} is primarily caused by failures in {target}'s installation
verbGlossary.CONDUCTED BY: {source} is physically performed and signed off by {target}
verbGlossary.WITNESSED BY: {source} is observed and co-signed by {target} as an independent verifier
verbGlossary.REPORTED BY: {source} is documented by {target} during a jobsite walkthrough
verbGlossary.TESTED ON: {source} documents testing performed on a sample of {target}
verbGlossary.SAMPLED FROM: {source} records a test specimen extracted from a batch of {target}
verbGlossary.BATCH-TESTED FOR: {source} covers a full batch acceptance test on {target} delivery
verbGlossary.CONTINUITY-TESTED ON: {source} includes continuity or resistance testing on {target}
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONED WITH: {source} is executed with {target} who installed the system
verbGlossary.PERFORMANCE-TESTED ON: {target} measures operational parameters of {source} under load
verbGlossary.PRESSURE-TESTED ON: {source} includes a pressure or hydrostatic test on {target}
verbGlossary.LOCKOUT-TAGGED ON: {source} issued for failure to follow lockout/tagout procedures on {target}
verbGlossary.CAUSED BY: {source} determines {target} was the proximate cause of the event
verbGlossary.INVOLVED IN INCIDENT WITH: {source} identifies {target} worker as directly involved in the safety event

_meta.universalEdgeSemantics: Each universalEdge means: the source element connects to EVERY element in the targetGroup via the specified verb. targetGroup values: 'trades' (25 elements), 'equipment' (11 elements), 'materials' (25 elements), 'measures' (9 elements). These groups correspond to the category sections in taxonomy-resources.csv.
_meta.dataEvidence: Universal relationships validated via Databricks queries against prod_gold_ram_us01 (2-year lookback, March 2026). Q1: safety obs × 30+ trades. Q2: punch items × all 16 MasterFormat divisions. Q3: submittals × all 18 material categories.
_meta.edgesVsUniversal: The 'edges' array contains SELECTIVE relationships — source elements that connect to specific targets only (e.g., material test reports apply to testable materials, not all). Universal edges are in 'universalEdges'.
